title: Your First R Code: Console and Scripts
description: Write and run your first R commands using the console and script files.

slides.title: Console vs Scripts 📜
slides.content:
## Console

The console (bottom-left in RStudio) is where you can type R commands one at a time. It's great for quick experiments and checking things.

## Scripts

A script is a text file with a `.R` extension where you write multiple lines of R code. You can save, edit, and rerun the entire script. This is essential for reproducible work.

In RStudio, create a new script via `File > New File > R Script` or click the green plus icon.

slides.title: Console in Action
slides.content: Open RStudio and type the following commands directly in the console, pressing Enter after each line:

quizData.question: Which of the following is true about R scripts?

options.text: They are best for one-time calculations
They allow you to save and reuse code

quizData.explanation: Scripts are saved files that contain code you can rerun anytime, making your analysis reproducible.

slides.title: Creating Your First Script ✍️
slides.content:
1. In RStudio, go to `File > New File > R Script`.
2. A new tab opens in the Source Editor.
3. Type the following lines:

```r
# My first script
name <- 'Alice'
print(paste('Hello,', name))
```

4. Save the script (Ctrl+S or Cmd+S) with a name like `hello.R`.

slides.title: Running a Script
slides.content:
There are several ways to run code from a script:

* To run a single line, place your cursor on that line and press `Ctrl+Enter` (Cmd+Enter on Mac).
* To run multiple lines, select them and press `Ctrl+Enter`.
* To run the entire script, click the **Source** button at the top of the script pane, or press `Ctrl+Shift+S`.

Try it now!
slides.codeSnippet:
x <- 10
y <- 20
x + y
# This is a comment and won't run

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## Using `source()`

You can also run an entire script from the console using the `source()` function:

```r
source('hello.R')
```

This is useful when you want to include scripts inside other scripts.

quizData.question: What keyboard shortcut runs the current line or selected code in RStudio?
quizData.explanation: Ctrl+Enter (Cmd+Enter on Mac) is the shortcut to run the current line or selection.

slides.title: Try It Yourself! 💪
slides.content: Create a new script and write code that calculates the average of three numbers: 12, 18, and 24. Save and run it.
slides.codeSnippet:
# Your code here
num1 <- 12
num2 <- 18
num3 <- 24
average <- (num1 + num2 + num3) / 3
print(average)
